title: Vendor protoc-gen-go-grpc stub generator from grpc-go (Apache 2.0)
description:
## Why

wiresmith today delegates service-stub generation to protoc-gen-go-grpc, which forces every adopter to install and invoke two separate codegen binaries (see Makefile lines around `--go-grpc_out`). Bringing stub emission into wiresmith gives a single-command codegen experience.

The official protoc-gen-go-grpc generator cannot be imported as a library — `google.golang.org/grpc/cmd/protoc-gen-go-grpc@v1.6.0/grpc.go` is `package main` (~635 LOC, Apache 2.0). Three real paths exist (subprocess the binary, vendor the source, write our own). Per direction from the wiresmith maintainer thread on 2026-06-05, this bead picks **vendor**: a single self-contained wiresmith binary owns the entire output.

The vendored file remains byte-identical to upstream where possible so future upstream syncs are a straight `cp` plus header reconciliation.

## What

1. Add `compiler/generator/grpc/` housing the vendored `grpc.go`. Preserve the Apache 2.0 licence header verbatim; add SPDX-License-Identifier. Update repo NOTICE / THIRD_PARTY (or create one mirroring `protohelpers/` precedent — that directory vendors vtprotobuf protohelpers under Apache 2.0, see how it documents attribution).
2. wiresmith uses `bufbuild/protocompile`, while the upstream generator expects `protogen.Plugin` / `protogen.File` / `protogen.GeneratedFile`. Bridge by either:
   - (a) Synthesising a `pluginpb.CodeGeneratorRequest` from wiresmith descriptors, running `protogen.Options{}.New(req)`, and calling the vendored `generateFileContent` unchanged. Minimal-edit path — preserves zero-cost upstream sync.
   - (b) Rewriting the vendored generator to talk to wiresmith descriptor types directly. Drops the protogen dep but loses sync ergonomics. Prefer (a) unless it hits a concrete obstacle.
3. Emit `*_grpc.pb.go` alongside existing wiresmith outputs, matching the split-file convention used by `_compare.pb.go` / `_equal.pb.go` / `_reflect.pb.go`. Trigger only when `len(file.Services) > 0`.
4. Wire into the wiresmith CLI / generator entry point and remove the standalone `protoc-gen-go-grpc` invocation from the Makefile once the new path is verified.
5. Rerun `test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go` against the wiresmith-emitted stubs — every test must pass (existing live-roundtrip suite: unary / client-stream / server-stream / bidi-stream / GetServiceInfo).

## Acceptance

- `gen/basic/service/v1/service_grpc.pb.go` is produced by `make generate-ours` with no `protoc-gen-go-grpc` invocation required.
- All five tests in `test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go` pass against the wiresmith-emitted stubs.
- Generated `service_grpc.pb.go` is byte-equivalent (or diff limited to file banner / package decoration) to what `protoc-gen-go-grpc@v1.6.0` emits today.
- `protoc-gen-go-grpc` dropped from Makefile and any install instructions / docs.
- Apache 2.0 licence header preserved verbatim in the vendored file; repo licence attribution updated.

## Out of scope

- Re-including `fdp.Service` in the embedded raw FileDescriptor — that is wiresmith-ea4 (descriptor-level reflection, separate concern). Note: see DESIGN below for the open question on whether ea4 must land first.
- Customising the gRPC codepath (mempool, Alloc-from-pool, custom codec à la vtprotobuf). Defer until a concrete user need exists.
- Tracking SupportPackageIsVersionN bumps. Stay pinned to v1.6.0 contract until forced to bump.

## Design open question

Once wiresmith emits its own `_grpc.pb.go`, the registration init() may need matching service entries in the embedded raw FileDescriptor — at minimum to avoid protoimpl.filetype.Builder.initDecls cardinality panics if the gRPC stubs reference protoreflect descriptors. If verification shows the panic triggers, this bead also un-strips `fdp.Service` (effectively subsuming wiresmith-ea4). If not, ea4 stays independent. Worth verifying early with a quick spike against the vendored generator output before committing to either dep direction.

## References

- `google.golang.org/grpc/cmd/protoc-gen-go-grpc@v1.6.0/grpc.go` — source to vendor (package main, Apache 2.0, ~635 LOC).
- `compiler/generator/emit_registration.go::serializeFileDescriptor` — current `fdp.Service = nil` strip site (left alone here; see Design open question above).
- `test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go` — live-roundtrip suite that validates new stubs.
- `Makefile` — `--go-grpc_out` invocation that this bead replaces.
- Competitor analysis: PR #105 (wiresmith-dei) and the wiresmith repo conversation on 2026-06-05 (vendor-vs-subprocess decision).
notes:
Design open question resolved: stripping fdp.Service in serializeFileDescriptor does NOT trigger a protoimpl.filetype.Builder.initDecls panic. Verified by TestGRPCProtocCompat_ServiceInfo (passes against wiresmith-emitted stubs). Reason: the gRPC stubs use grpc.ServiceDesc registration via Server.GetServiceInfo, independent of protoreflect — they reference message types whose ProtoReflect() lives in wiresmith but never walk service entries on the file descriptor. wiresmith-ea4 (un-strip fdp.Service for descriptor-level reflection) stays an independent bead.
Output diff vs upstream protoc-gen-go-grpc@v1.6.0 (3 lines, all in non-code regions): (1) banner protoc version 'v7.35.0' → '(unknown)' (no CompilerVersion synthesized in CodeGeneratorRequest), (2) banner 'source:' path 'service.proto' → 'basic/service/v1/service.proto', (3) Echo_ServiceDesc.Metadata field same path change. (2) and (3) reflect wiresmith's synthetic import-key path (proto-package-derived) vs protoc's invocation-relative path. Metadata is informational (used by grpc reflection responses); behavioral impact verified zero by the 5 test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go tests.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/116
close_reason: implemented

title: Loki migration
description:
## Goal

Replace `protoc-gen-gogoslick` (and `protoc-gen-go-grpc` for some packages) as the protobuf compiler for [grafana/loki](https://github.com/grafana/loki) with `wiresmith`. This enables Loki to benefit from wiresmith's value-type structs, lower allocations, and faster (de)serialization on the hot ingest/query paths — most critically `pkg/push/push.proto` (every `PushRequest`) and `pkg/engine/internal/proto/{physicalpb,wirepb}/*.proto` (engine scheduler/worker hot path).

## Why

Loki's hottest data paths are `PushRequest`/`StreamAdapter` (ingest) and the new engine's `Plan`/`Task`/`Frame` types (query execution). Both go through gogofast-generated marshaling today. Loki is also actively building the new engine layer (the recent `IndexMerge` node marshalling work, `chore(engine): Add stats for measuring data locality`, etc.) and adopting wiresmith there gives the largest perf headroom while the API surface is still internal.

## Scope of the migration

Counts from `grafana/loki` (main, 2026-06-05):

- **38 `.proto` files** under `pkg/**`, all `proto3`.
- **24 files** import `gogoproto/gogo.proto` and rely on at least one gogoproto extension.
- **15 files** declare gRPC `service` definitions covering every Loki transport: querier, ingester (push), index-gateway, bloom-gateway, scheduler, frontend v1/v2, compactor, ruler, bloombuild, limits, pattern.
- **7 files** use streaming RPCs (`returns (stream X)`): pattern, indexgateway, logproto (3 streams), ingester-rf1, scheduler, bloombuild, compactor, frontend v1.

### gogoproto extensions used by Loki's .proto files

Collected with `grep -h "gogoproto\.[a-z_]*" $(find pkg -name '*.proto')`:

| gogoproto option            | Count | Wiresmith status                                            |
| --------------------------- | -----:| ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `jsontag`                   |   237 | **native** — `(wiresmith.options.jsontag)`                  |
| `nullable`                  |   187 | native — wiresmith defaults to value-types; pointer via `(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true` |
| `customtype` on bytes/string |  ~5 | [[wiresmith-06z]] — shipped                                |
| `customtype` on message/scalar | ~75 | [[wiresmith-hnr]] — **OPEN, critical for `push.Stream`, `ulid.ULID`, `model.Time`, `LabelAdapter`** |
| `stdtime`                   |    54 | [[wiresmith-gqx]] — shipped                                |
| `marshaler_all` / `unmarshaler_all` / `sizer_all` | 31 | native — wiresmith always emits Marshal/Unmarshal/Size |
| `stdduration`               |     8 | [[wiresmith-b34]] — open                                   |
| `casttype`                  |     5 | [[wiresmith-cr0]] — open                                   |
| `customname`                |     1 | [[wiresmith-boe]] — shipped                                |

### Well-known types

- `google.protobuf.Timestamp` — 17 files. Covered by `(wiresmith.options.stdtime)` [[wiresmith-gqx]] ✓
- `google.protobuf.Duration` — 5 files (`physicalpb.proto`, `ruler/base/ruler.proto`, `ruler/rulespb/rules.proto`, `querier/stats/stats.proto`, `querier/queryrange/queryrange.proto`). Needs `(wiresmith.options.stdduration)` [[wiresmith-b34]].
- `google.protobuf.Any` — 2 files. [[wiresmith-7m6]]:
  - `pkg/ruler/rulespb/rules.proto` (rule definitions)
  - `pkg/storage/chunk/cache/resultscache/types.proto` (cached query response payload — hot path)
- `google.protobuf.Empty` — 1 file. `pkg/compactor/client/grpc/grpc.proto` (single use; could be replaced with empty message).

### gRPC services

15 Loki proto files declare RPCs. Covered by [[wiresmith-36b]] (closed via [[wiresmith-dei]]: protoc-gen-go-grpc reuses wiresmith messages directly). Streaming RPCs covered too.

[[wiresmith-ea4]] is needed if any consumer of Loki's gRPC services uses protoreflect-based reflection (e.g. `grpc_reflection_v1alpha`), since wiresmith strips `fdp.Service` and `Services().Len()` reports 0.

## Loki-specific structural blockers (not yet covered by any bead)

These are blockers unique to Loki's proto layout. Filed as sibling beads off this epic — see DEPENDS ON list.

### Same proto `package` ↔ multiple `go_package` (HARD blocker)

Wiresmith rejects compilation when one `.proto` `package` name maps to multiple Go packages (`compiler/generator/generator.go:559`). Loki violates this three times:

| proto `package`  | go_packages                                                                                                                                       |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `logproto`       | `github.com/grafana/loki/pkg/push` (no v3 — separate go-module) + `github.com/grafana/loki/v3/pkg/logproto` (8 files split across two directories) |
| `proto`          | `pkg/dataobj/compaction/proto`, `pkg/dataobj/compaction/v2/proto`, `pkg/limits/proto`                                                              |
| `stats`          | `pkg/logqlmodel/stats`, `pkg/querier/stats`                                                                                                        |

The `logproto` split is load-bearing: `pkg/push` is a separately-versioned Go module so external clients can depend on the push API without all of Loki. The other two are cosmetic.

**Options:** (a) Loki renames `package proto;` and `package stats;` to unique names per directory (cosmetic — only `(gogoproto.casttype)`-style FQN consumers care). (b) `logproto` requires either re-coupling `pkg/push` into the main module or accepting a proto-package rename on the wire (low risk — no Loki wire bytes embed the FQN, but external clients using `proto.MessageName` could break). (c) Wiresmith relaxes the rule to allow per-file `go_package` override matching protoc's `paths=source_relative`.

### Sibling-style proto imports (MINOR blocker)

`pkg/storage/chunk/cache/resultscache/test_types.proto` uses `import "types.proto";` to reference a sibling — works under protoc because Loki's Makefile passes `-I ./$(@D)`. Wiresmith resolves imports only against `--proto_path`. Either rewrite the import to its fully-qualified path, or use `--proto_path` per package. [[wiresmith-zxd]] addresses the multi-root case generally.

### Duplicate `service PusherRF1` (Loki bug surfaced by wiresmith)

`service PusherRF1 { ... }` is defined twice — `pkg/push/push-rf1.proto:11` and `pkg/logproto/ingester-rf1.proto:12`, both with `package logproto;`. Protoc tolerates this with per-file compilation; wiresmith errors with `symbol "logproto.PusherRF1" already defined`. This is a real Loki bug to fix during migration.

## Loki-side work not covered by wiresmith beads

After all wiresmith deps land, the Loki-side migration still needs:

1. **Resolve the `proto package` collisions** (see structural blockers above). Likely a rename pass on `package proto` / `package stats` and a careful decision on `package logproto`.
2. **Adapter types for customtype.** `push.Stream`, `ulid.ULID`, `model.Time` (`model.Fingerprint`), `syntax.LineFilter`, `plan.QueryPlan`, `pkg/engine/internal/util/dag.Graph`-related types all currently implement gogo-shaped `{Marshal,MarshalTo,MarshalToSizedBuffer,Unmarshal,Size}`. Wiresmith customtype expects `{Size,Marshal,Unmarshal,Equal,Compare}Wiresmith` (per [[wiresmith-06z]] notes). Needs new methods on those types or wrapper adapters.
3. **Rewriting `.proto` annotations.** Mechanical sweep over `(gogoproto.*)` → `(wiresmith.options.*)`. [[wiresmith-93t]] would eliminate this if it lands.
4. **Replace `google.protobuf.Empty` with a defined message** in `pkg/compactor/client/grpc/grpc.proto` (or wait on [[wiresmith-7m6]] for full WKT support).
5. **Drop legacy `pkg/ruler/base/`** if possible — it's gogofast-generated, uses `stdtime`, and is in maintenance mode. Less migration work if removed.
6. **Update `Makefile`'s `protos:` target** to invoke wiresmith instead of `protoc --gogoslick_out`. Drop the per-file `protoc` invocation; switch to per-package wiresmith calls with explicit `--proto_path` roots.
7. **`clean-protos:` glob update** — wiresmith emits 4 sibling files per `.proto` (`*.pb.go`, `*_compare.pb.go`, `*_equal.pb.go`, `*_reflect.pb.go`); current rule only removes `*.pb.go`.

## Hard blockers, in dependency order

1. **`customtype` on message and scalar fields** — [[wiresmith-hnr]] (open, P1) — used 80x across Loki:
   - `pkg/logproto/logproto.proto` (23): `push.Stream`, `model.Time` (`from`/`through`), `plan.QueryPlan`, `syntax.LineFilter`, `LabelAdapter`.
   - `pkg/querier/queryrange/queryrange.proto` (24): same set.
   - `pkg/engine/internal/proto/wirepb/wirepb.proto` (8): `ulid.ULID`.
   - `pkg/compactor/deletion/deletionproto/types.proto` (6), `pkg/logproto/bloomgateway.proto` (5), `pkg/logproto/metrics.proto` (3).
2. **`stdduration`** — [[wiresmith-b34]] (open, P2) — 5 files. Critical for `pkg/engine/internal/proto/physicalpb/physicalpb.proto` (`AggregateRange.Step/Range`, hot path).
3. **`casttype`** — [[wiresmith-cr0]] (open, P2) — 5 sites: `model.Fingerprint` in `indexgateway.proto`, `bloombuild/protos/types.proto`, `DetectedFieldType` in `logproto.proto`.
4. **`google.protobuf.Any`** — [[wiresmith-7m6]] (open, P2) — 2 files. `resultscache/types.proto` is in the query-result cache hot path; can't be reserved out.
5. **Multi `--proto_path` support** — [[wiresmith-zxd]] (open, P2) — to handle vendor/ + sibling-style imports without restructuring the Loki tree.
6. **(Loki-internal) Resolve the `proto package` ↔ `go_package` 1-to-1 collisions** — see structural blockers above.

## Per-file blocker matrix (38 files)

**Migratable today (no open blockers, only annotation rewriting):**
- `pkg/logqlmodel/stats/stats.proto`
- `pkg/dataobj/compaction/proto/compaction.proto` (after `package` rename)
- `pkg/dataobj/compaction/v2/proto/compactionv2.proto` (after `package` rename)
- `pkg/dataobj/metastore/metastore.proto`
- `pkg/dataobj/internal/metadata/datasetmd/datasetmd.proto`
- `pkg/dataobj/internal/metadata/filemd/filemd.proto`
- `pkg/xcap/internal/proto/xcap.proto`
- `pkg/engine/internal/proto/ulid/ulid.proto`
- `pkg/engine/internal/proto/expressionpb/expressionpb.proto`
- `pkg/limits/proto/limits.proto` (after `package` rename — needs `wiresmith-dei` recipe for service)
- `clients/cmd/docker-driver/logdriver/entry.proto` (already protoc-gen-go)

**Service-only (need protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse path, already proven via wiresmith-dei):**
- `pkg/lokifrontend/frontend/v2/frontendv2pb/frontend.proto`

**Blocked by hnr (customtype-on-messages) only:**
- `pkg/logproto/sketch.proto`
- `pkg/logproto/ingester-rf1.proto` (after fixing PusherRF1 duplicate)
- `pkg/push/push-rf1.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + service:**
- `pkg/push/push.proto`
- `pkg/logproto/bloomgateway.proto`
- `pkg/logproto/metrics.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + cr0:**
- `pkg/bloombuild/protos/types.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + cr0 + service:**
- `pkg/logproto/indexgateway.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + b34 + cr0 + service:**
- `pkg/logproto/logproto.proto` ← largest blocker concentration

**Blocked by hnr + b34 only:**
- `pkg/engine/internal/proto/physicalpb/physicalpb.proto` ← engine hot path
- `pkg/querier/queryrange/queryrange.proto`
- `pkg/querier/queryrange/queryrangebase/queryrange.proto`

**Blocked by hnr only (engine):**
- `pkg/engine/internal/proto/wirepb/wirepb.proto`

**Blocked by b34 only:**
- `pkg/querier/stats/stats.proto`
- `pkg/bloombuild/protos/service.proto` (also service)
- `pkg/querier/queryrange/queryrangebase/definitions/definitions.proto`

**Blocked by 7m6 (Any):**
- `pkg/storage/chunk/cache/resultscache/types.proto` (also imported by everyone)
- `pkg/ruler/rulespb/rules.proto`

**Blocked by 7m6 (Empty) + service:**
- `pkg/compactor/client/grpc/grpc.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + b34 + service (legacy):**
- `pkg/ruler/base/ruler.proto` ← consider deleting outright (legacy ruler)

**Blocked by hnr + service:**
- `pkg/scheduler/schedulerpb/scheduler.proto`
- `pkg/lokifrontend/frontend/v1/frontendv1pb/frontend.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + checkpoint customtype:**
- `pkg/ingester/checkpoint.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + service + Loki PusherRF1 dup fix:**
- `pkg/logproto/pattern.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + casttype + resultscache:**
- `pkg/storage/chunk/cache/resultscache/test_types.proto` (also fix sibling import)

**Blocked by hnr + b34:**
- `pkg/compactor/deletion/deletionproto/types.proto`

## Out of scope (until enough is unblocked to pilot)

- Per-package migration sequencing — wait until at least `wiresmith-hnr` and `wiresmith-b34` land.
- Benchmark comparison against gogoslick on `PushRequest` / engine hot path — done as part of the migration PR.
- Any deletion of the `gogoproto` vendor directory.
- Migrating `clients/cmd/docker-driver/` (one-off, separate concern).

## Definition of done

- Every Loki `.pb.go` file currently produced by `gogoslick` can be regenerated by wiresmith with byte-identical wire compatibility on the `PushRequest` / `QueryResponse` / engine `Frame` paths.
- `make protos` in `grafana/loki` uses wiresmith.
- All Loki unit + integration tests pass against the wiresmith-generated code.
- A benchmark comparison vs. the gogoslick baseline on `pkg/push` and `pkg/engine/internal/proto/wirepb` is published in the PR.
- Backwards-compatible JSON encoding for everything the HTTP API touches (Volume/IndexStats/Stream/Sample/Series response shapes) — verified by golden tests in `pkg/util/marshal/`.

title: Tempo migration
description:
Replace gogofaster / protoc-gen-go-grpc with wiresmith as the protobuf compiler in [grafana/tempo](https://github.com/grafana/tempo).

## Scope

The Tempo repo has four proto trees that need wiresmith codegen:

| Proto | Generator today | Wiresmith-only blocker | Migration scope |
|-------|-----------------|------------------------|-----------------|
| `pkg/.patched-proto/{common,resource,trace}/v1/*.proto` (patched OTel) | gogofaster | none — pure proto3 messages | Migration-ready once wiresmith CLI is wired into Makefile. |
| `pkg/tempopb/tempo.proto` | gogofaster + grpc | gRPC services + streaming RPCs + `stdtime`/`customtype`/`nullable` | Full feature set required. |
| `pkg/tempopb/backendwork.proto` | gogofaster + grpc | gRPC services + per-message `equal`/`compare`/`marshaler_all`/`unmarshaler_all` | Full feature set required. |
| `modules/frontend/v1/frontendv1pb/frontend.proto` | gogofaster + grpc | gRPC services + bidi streaming (`stream X returns stream Y`) | Full feature set required. |
| `tempodb/backend/v1/v1.proto` | gogofaster | `embed` + `customtype` + `customname` + `jsontag` + `stdtime` (heavy gogoproto, no gRPC) | Storage-layer; touches every block-read/write path. |

## Required wiresmith features (blockers below)

Confirmed by reading the Tempo proto files (`gogoproto.*` annotations + `service`/`rpc` blocks):

- gRPC service stub generation incl. streaming — `wiresmith-36b` (umbrella).
- `(wiresmith.options.stdtime)` for `google.protobuf.Timestamp` → `time.Time` — `wiresmith-gqx`.
- `(wiresmith.options.customtype)` for `PreallocBytes`, `UUID`, `Encoding`, `DedicatedColumn` — `wiresmith-06z`.
- `(wiresmith.options.jsontag)` for the HTTP API tag stability in `tempodb/backend/v1` — `wiresmith-gd2` (new).
- `(wiresmith.options.customname)` for `BlockID`/`TenantID`-style initialism-preserving names — `wiresmith-boe` (new).
- `(wiresmith.options.embed)` for `CompactedBlockMeta.BlockMeta` inlining — `wiresmith-9ks` (new).
- `Compare()` method generation for `backendwork` scheduler ordering — `wiresmith-2xv` (new).

`nullable = false` is already the wiresmith default (value-type fields); `marshaler_all`/`unmarshaler_all` are functionally equivalent to wiresmith's always-on marshal/unmarshal; per-message `equal` is covered by the existing nil-safe `Equal()`.

## Out of scope for this epic

- Migrating Mimir / other Grafana consumers — separate epic per repo.
- Removing the gogo runtime dependency from Tempo entirely (some non-proto callers still pull `gogo/protobuf/types`). Treated as a follow-up.
- Performance evaluation versus the current gogofaster build — done as part of the migration PR, not a precondition.

## Plan (high level)

1. Land all blocker beads in wiresmith.
2. In Tempo: rewrite `(gogoproto.*)` annotations to their `(wiresmith.options.*)` equivalents (or rely on the alias-recognition each blocker bead defines).
3. Swap `buf/buf.gen.*.yaml` `protoc-gen-gogofaster` for `wiresmith` invocations; regenerate `*.pb.go` and `*_grpc.pb.go`.
4. Drop `vendor/github.com/gogo/protobuf` from the proto-codegen path.
5. Run `make test` + `make integration` + benchmarks; verify wire compatibility against canned blocks from prior versions.

## Acceptance criteria

- `make gen-proto` in Tempo runs wiresmith only — no `protoc-gen-gogofaster` or `protoc-gen-go-grpc` invocations.
- All existing unit, integration, and benchmark tests pass with the regenerated code.
- Backwards-compatible JSON encoding for `tempodb/backend/v1` block metadata (no regressions in stored block decoding).
- CHANGELOG entry documenting the swap and the floor wiresmith version pinned.

title: Mimir migration
description:
## Goal

Replace `protoc-gen-gogoslick` (and `protoc-gen-go-grpc` for some packages) as the protobuf compiler for [grafana/mimir](https://github.com/grafana/mimir) with `wiresmith`. This enables Mimir to benefit from wiresmith's value-type structs, lower allocations, and faster (de)serialization on the hot write/query paths — most critically `pkg/mimirpb/mimir.proto`, which carries every `WriteRequest`.

## Why

Mimir's hottest data structures (`PreallocTimeseries`, label sets, samples, histograms, exemplars) flow through gogoproto-generated marshaling. The Mimir codebase is also the strategic Grafana consumer the wiresmith README implicitly targets: replacing gogoproto on a production multi-tenant ingester is the proof point that wiresmith can replace gogofaster outside the OTel collector.

## Scope of the migration

Counts from `grafana/mimir` (branch `wiresmith`, 2026-06-05):

- **33 `.proto` files** under `pkg/**`, all `proto3`.
- **29 files** import `gogoproto/gogo.proto` and rely on at least one gogoproto extension.
- **10 files** declare gRPC `service` definitions (ingester, ruler, scheduler, store-gateway, alertmanager, distributor, frontend v2, usage-tracker, block-builder scheduler, compactor scheduler).

### gogoproto extensions used by Mimir's .proto files

Collected with `grep -rh "gogoproto\.[a-z_]*" pkg/**/*.proto`:

| gogoproto option            | Wiresmith status                                            |
| --------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `customtype` on bytes/string | [[wiresmith-06z]] — shipped                                |
| `customtype` on message     | [[wiresmith-hnr]] — **OPEN, critical for PreallocTimeseries/LabelAdapter** |
| `casttype`                  | [[wiresmith-cr0]] — open                                   |
| `stdtime`                   | [[wiresmith-gqx]] — shipped                                |
| `stdduration`               | [[wiresmith-b34]] — open                                   |
| `goproto_enum_prefix`       | [[wiresmith-1dq]] — open (cosmetic — workaround is to rename enum values in proto) |
| `marshaler_all` / `unmarshaler_all` / `sizer_all` | native — wiresmith always emits Marshal/Unmarshal/Size |
| `nullable`                  | native — wiresmith defaults to value-types; pointer via `(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true` |
| `equal_all`                 | **native** — wiresmith emits `Equal()` in `<name>_equal.pb.go` |
| `jsontag`                   | **native** — `(wiresmith.options.jsontag)`                  |
| `messagename_all`           | **no callers** in Mimir non-generated code; safe to drop the option |
| `goproto_getters_all`       | **native** — wiresmith always emits `Get<Field>()`         |
| `goproto_sizecache_all`     | **no-op** — wiresmith has no `XXX_sizecache` field         |
| `goproto_unkeyed_all`       | **no-op** — wiresmith doesn't add `XXX_unrecognized`-style fields by default |
| `goproto_unrecognized_all`  | [[wiresmith-4je]] — relevant only if a consumer needs gogo `proto.Buffer` compat (Mimir does NOT — see `pkg/util/http.go:162` falls through to `Unmarshal` directly) |

### Well-known types

Mimir uses well-known types in:

- `google.protobuf.Timestamp` — covered by `(wiresmith.options.stdtime)` [[wiresmith-gqx]] ✓
- `google.protobuf.Duration` — needs `(wiresmith.options.stdduration)` [[wiresmith-b34]]
- `google.protobuf.Any` — **only [[wiresmith-7m6]]**. Used in 4 files:
  - `pkg/ruler/rulespb/rules.proto`: `repeated google.protobuf.Any options` (ruler Prometheus Manager opts, cold path)
  - `pkg/frontend/querymiddleware/model.proto`: `google.protobuf.Any response` (query-result cache extent)
  - `pkg/scheduler/schedulerpb/scheduler.proto`: `google.protobuf.Any payload` (scheduler protocol)
  - `pkg/storegateway/storepb/rpc.proto`: 5 uses, **all on Deprecated fields** — could be reserved+removed instead of migrated

### gRPC services

10 Mimir proto files declare RPCs. Covered by [[wiresmith-36b]] (closed via [[wiresmith-dei]]: protoc-gen-go-grpc reuses wiresmith messages directly).

## Mimir-side work not covered by wiresmith beads

After all wiresmith deps land, the Mimir-side migration still needs:

1. **Adapter types for customtype.** `PreallocTimeseries` (`pkg/mimirpb/timeseries.go:143`) and `LabelAdapter` (`pkg/mimirpb/timeseries.go:520`) currently implement gogo-shaped `{Marshal,MarshalTo,MarshalToSizedBuffer,Unmarshal,Size}`. wiresmith customtype expects `{Size,Marshal,Unmarshal,Equal,Compare}Wiresmith` (per [[wiresmith-06z]] notes). Mimir needs either new methods on those types or wrapper adapter types.

2. **Rewriting `.proto` annotations.** `(gogoproto.customtype)` is not aliased to `(wiresmith.options.customtype)` ([[wiresmith-06z]] deferred this — see new bead for the alias). Every `.proto` file with gogoproto annotations must be rewritten by hand to use `wiresmith.options.*` equivalents.

3. **Updating the `apply-expected-diffs.sh` patches.** The `*.pb.go.expdiff` files in Mimir are gogoslick-output-specific and become invalid under wiresmith.

4. **Cross-package import paths.** `mimir.proto` uses `go_package = "mimirpb"` (bare name). Wiresmith honors `go_package` literally; needs to be either changed to the full path or overridden via `-M`.

## Hard blockers, in dependency order

1. **`customtype` on message fields** — [[wiresmith-hnr]] (open, P1) — `WriteRequest.timeseries` (`PreallocTimeseries`), `TimeSeries.labels`/`Metric.labels`/`Exemplar.labels` (`UnsafeMutableLabel`/`LabelAdapter`). Blocks `mimir.proto`, `ingester.proto`, `ruler.proto`, `querierpb`, `frontendv2pb`, `storegateway/storepb/{cache,types}.proto`, `planning/core/core.proto`, `rulespb/rules.proto`, `querymiddleware/model.proto`.
2. **`casttype`** — [[wiresmith-cr0]] (open, P2) — `FloatHistogram.counter_reset_hint`, `streamingpromql/planning/plan.proto` and `planning/core/core.proto`.
3. **`stdduration`** — [[wiresmith-b34]] (open, P2) — `ruler.proto`, `querier/stats/stats.proto`, several `streamingpromql/optimize/plan/*` files, `planning/{plan,core/core}.proto`, `rulespb/rules.proto`.
4. **`google.protobuf.Any`** — [[wiresmith-7m6]] (open, P2) — 4 files. Storegateway uses are all on deprecated fields and can be reserved-out instead.
5. **`goproto_enum_prefix`** (cosmetic only) — [[wiresmith-1dq]] (open, P3). Mimir can rename `Histogram.ResetHint` values to embed the prefix manually as a cheap workaround.

## Per-file blocker matrix (33 files)

**Migratable today (no open blockers, only annotation rewriting):**
- `pkg/distributor/ha_tracker.proto`
- `pkg/streamingpromql/types/types.proto`
- `pkg/streamingpromql/operators/functions/functions.proto`
- `pkg/storage/indexheader/indexheaderpb/sparse.proto`
- `pkg/alertmanager/alertspb/alerts.proto` (already protoc-gen-go)
- `pkg/storegateway/hintspb/hints.proto`
- `pkg/streamingpromql/optimize/plan/rangevectorsplitting/functions.proto`
- `pkg/streamingpromql/optimize/plan/rangevectorsplitting/cache/cache.proto`
- `pkg/streamingpromql/optimize/plan/commonsubexpressionelimination/node.proto`
- `pkg/streamingpromql/optimize/plan/multiaggregation/node.proto`
- `pkg/streamingpromql/optimize/plan/remoteexec/node.proto`

**Service-only (need protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse path, already proven via wiresmith-dei):**
- `pkg/distributor/distributorpb/distributor.proto`
- `pkg/blockbuilder/schedulerpb/scheduler.proto`
- `pkg/compactor/scheduler/compactorschedulerpb/compactorscheduler.proto`
- `pkg/usagetracker/usagetrackerpb/usagetracker.proto`
- `pkg/storegateway/storegatewaypb/gateway.proto`
- `pkg/alertmanager/alertmanagerpb/alertmanager.proto` (already)

**Blocked by hnr only:**
- `pkg/querier/querierpb/querier.proto`
- `pkg/storegateway/storepb/cache.proto`
- `pkg/storegateway/storepb/types.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + service:**
- `pkg/frontend/v2/frontendv2pb/frontend.proto`
- `pkg/ingester/client/ingester.proto`

**Blocked by b34 only:**
- `pkg/querier/stats/stats.proto`
- `pkg/streamingpromql/optimize/plan/rangevectorsplitting/node.proto`
- `pkg/streamingpromql/optimize/plan/splitandcache/node.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + cr0 + b34 + 1dq:**
- `pkg/mimirpb/mimir.proto` ← already partially migrated on this branch
- `pkg/streamingpromql/planning/core/core.proto`

**Blocked by cr0 + b34:**
- `pkg/streamingpromql/planning/plan.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + b34 + service:**

**Blocked by hnr + 7m6 (Any):**

**Blocked by 7m6 (Any) only — but all Any uses are on Deprecated fields:**

## Out of scope (until enough is unblocked to pilot)

- Per-package migration sequencing.
- Benchmark comparison against the current gogoslick output on `WriteRequest` hot path.
- Any deletion of gogoproto vendor.

## Definition of done

- Every Mimir `.pb.go` file currently produced by `gogoslick` can be regenerated by wiresmith with byte-identical wire compatibility on the WriteRequest / ingester / ruler paths.
- `make protos` in `grafana/mimir` uses wiresmith.
- All Mimir unit + integration tests pass against the wiresmith-generated code.
- A benchmark comparison vs. the gogoslick baseline on `pkg/mimirpb` is published.

title: SEC-3: 32-bit length-decode truncation in skipValue and inline helper
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:32-43` (skipValue case 2); `compiler/types/type.go:227-232` (`emitConsumeBytesLenAt`).

**What:** `byteLen` is decoded into `uint64` but immediately converted with `int(byteLen)` and checked only for `< 0`. On `GOARCH=386/arm/wasm` (32-bit `int`), values in `(MaxInt32, MaxInt64]` truncate to small positive ints, bypassing both the `< 0` check and the `postIndex > l` bound. CLAUDE.md "Common review caveats" lists this exact pitfall under "32-bit length safety".

**Fix:** Before `int(byteLen)`, add `if byteLen > uint64(math.MaxInt) { return io.ErrUnexpectedEOF }`. Apply in both `skipValue` and `emitConsumeBytesLenAt`. Add a test that runs under `GOARCH=386`.
close_reason: merged via #63 (commit 9e26682) — bead missed by earlier sweep

title: CR-4: Self-referencing message without optional emits infinite Go struct
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_struct.go:51` (no cycle detection); generator emits value-type message fields by default.

**What:** `message Tree { Tree child = 1; }` is valid proto3. wiresmith emits `Child Tree` (value type), which `go vet` and `go build` both reject as a recursively-sized struct. The pointer-option (`(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true`) and `optional` keyword both fix it, but the generator silently emits the broken struct rather than detecting the cycle.

**Fix:** Add a SCC-based cycle check during planning, following only non-optional/non-repeated/non-pointer-option/non-oneof/non-map edges. On detection, return an actionable error pointing at the offending field and suggesting `optional` or `(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true`.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: SEC-2: Pre-scan default: break only exits switch, not outer loop
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:138-141`.

**What:** When `preTyp ∈ {3,4,6,7}` (legacy groups + reserved), the `default: break` only terminates the inner `switch`; `preIdx` is unchanged, so the next iteration treats payload bytes as a new tag. This inflates field counts and feeds SEC-1. The post-switch guard at line 141 only catches genuinely out-of-bounds `preIdx`.

**Fix:** Either label the outer `for` and `break OUTER`, or set `preIdx = l` in the default branch, or simply abort the pre-scan entirely on unknown wire type (the main loop is the source of truth — pre-scan is an optimisation). The "abort the optimisation" option is safest.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: CR-3: Empty .proto produces uncompilable Go
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/generator.go:271-309` (`generateFile`); pertinent imports added unconditionally at `emit_unmarshal.go:163-165`.

**What:** A `syntax = "proto3"; package empty;` with no messages or enums still emits a `.pb.go` that imports `protohelpers`/`protowire` but never uses them. CLAUDE.md flags this as a known caveat; nothing in the generator guards it.

**Fix:** In `generateFile`, short-circuit if `messages == 0 && enums == 0` — either skip the file entirely or emit only the package clause. Add a generator test with an empty fixture proto.
close_reason: Closed

title: CR-2: Negative-zero floats stripped on marshal (silent corruption)
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/types/fixed64.go:42-43` (`EmitSize`, `EmitMarshal` use `if access != 0`); `compiler/types/fixed32.go:43` mirrors this for `float`.

**What:** In Go, `-0.0 != 0` is `false`, so a singular `double`/`float` set to `-0.0` is treated as a default value and elided from the wire. On round-trip it reads back as `+0.0`. `math.Signbit` is lost. The official `google.golang.org/protobuf` library guards this with `if v == 0 && !math.Signbit(v) { return }` (see its `codec_gen.go`).

**Fix:** Emit `if math.Float64bits(access) != 0` (or, equivalently, `Float32bits`) so any non-zero bit pattern — including negative zero — serialises. Add a unit test in `test/basic/numeric_test.go` that marshals `math.Copysign(0, -1)` and asserts `math.Signbit` survives the round-trip.
close_reason: Fix: marshal/size now use math.FloatNNbits(v) != 0 so -0.0 survives the wire round-trip; matches google.golang.org/protobuf. Includes test coverage in test/basic/numeric_test.go.

title: SEC-1: Pre-scan slice pre-allocation is attacker-amplifiable
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:144-156` (pre-scan size hint), `99-141` (pre-scan loop).

**What:** The pre-scan counts field-number occurrences then does `m.X = make([]Span, 0, count)`. A malicious 1 MB payload of tiny tag-for-field-1 + zero-length entries can produce ~500 000 occurrences, allocating ~125 MB of capacity for `[]Span` (struct size ≈ 250 B). Combined with SEC-2 below, the count itself becomes attacker-controlled.

**Fix:** Cap pre-allocated capacity by a sanity bound: `cap = min(count, (l-iNdEx)/minBytesPerElement)` where `minBytesPerElement` is the smallest legal wire encoding for the element type (e.g., `1` for varint scalar tag + minimum body). For message-typed repeated fields, the minimum is 2 bytes (tag + zero-length). Easy formula: `cap = min(count, l/2)`. Add a fuzz seed that exercises this exact shape.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: CR-1: Reset() panics on nil receiver
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_reset.go:15`

**What:** Emits `func (m *T) Reset() { *m = T{} }` with no nil guard, while the same generator emits `String()` *with* a nil guard at line 17–19. `CLAUDE.md` "Common review caveats" lists `Reset()` as one of the methods that must handle nil gracefully — this regressed silently because `String()` got the treatment and `Reset()` did not.

**Fix:** `func (m *T) Reset() { if m == nil { return }; *m = T{} }`. One branch on a cold path; trivially testable.
close_reason: merged via #64 (commit 28613ea); bead never got closed at the time

title: Collapse four field-option pipelines into a single registry
description: Per PR 3 of the wiresmith-gqx simplification plan. Depends on wiresmith-0fa. Replace pointerExt/customtypeExt/customnameExt/stdtimeExt cache fields, the per-option resolve<X>Extension/validate<X>Options/has<X>Option/<x>FieldType/<x>GoFieldType helpers, and the if-cascade in fieldType()/goFieldType() with a single FieldOption interface and a Generator.options slice. New file compiler/generator/option.go with FieldOption interface (Name, Resolve, Validate, FieldType, GoFieldType). Each option_<name>.go becomes a struct with methods. Pays off when stdduration/stdfields land (more options confirmed coming). Verification: make generate-ours produces zero diff against baseline; all option_*_test.go pass unchanged.
close_reason: PR 3 implemented: FieldOption interface + registry (newOptionRegistry) replaces the four parallel resolve<X>Extension / validate<X>Options / <x>FieldType / <x>GoFieldType pipelines. Each option_<name>.go is now a struct implementing Name / Resolve / Validate / FieldType / GoFieldType. The eight inline Generate calls collapse into two registry loops. Cross-option dependency (stdtime needs pointer) routes via findOption[T]. jsontag stays outside the registry (no FieldType/GoFieldType behavior). All tests pass; make generate-ours produces zero diff; conformance 700/2; fuzz 30s green. Future options (stdduration, stdfields, anypb) just register one struct.

title: Collapse g.dests + destsByPath into single fd.Path()-keyed map
description: Per PR 2 of the wiresmith-gqx simplification plan. Depends on wiresmith-pc0. Remove the proto-package-keyed destination map (g.dests). Every real lookup already has access to a *FileDescriptor (md.ParentFile()/ed.ParentFile()); the legacy 'by proto package only' fallback is unused. Rename and unify on fd.Path()-keyed map. Drops the zero-goDest sentinel for fall-through. Updates ImportTracker, addProtoImport, newImportTracker signatures and removes resolvePkgName/selfPkg. Verification: make generate-ours produces zero diff against baseline (refactor is import-resolution dispatch only).
close_reason: PR 2 implemented: g.dests and g.destsByPath collapsed into single g.destinations map (keyed by fd.Path()). ImportTracker now carries selfDest + destinations instead of selfPkg + dests + destsByPath. goDest gained protoPkg field to preserve the proto-package-derived alias fallback in addProtoImport. resolvePkgName helper removed (callers use selfDest.pkgName directly). All tests pass; make generate-ours produces zero diff; conformance 700/2.

title: Move stdtime helpers from per-file emission into protohelpers/
description: Per PR 1 of the wiresmith-gqx simplification plan. Delete compiler/generator/emit_stdtime.go and the per-file sizeStdTime/encodeStdTime/decodeStdTime emission. Add SizeStdTime/EncodeStdTime/DecodeStdTime to protohelpers/. Generated _stdtime_*.pb.go shrinks by ~140 LOC. DecodeStdTime is rewritten to use google.golang.org/protobuf/encoding/protowire (ConsumeTag, ConsumeVarint, ConsumeFieldValue) — removes the hand-inlined varint loop and removes the dependency on per-file skipValue. SizeStdTime/EncodeStdTime keep their current logic (no perf-relevant rewrite). PR 1 does not change wire-format behavior.
close_reason: PR 1 implemented: stdtime helpers (SizeStdTime/EncodeStdTime/DecodeStdTime) now live in protohelpers/. Generated stdtime.pb.go dropped from ~550 to 398 LOC. Decoder uses protowire.ConsumeTag/ConsumeVarint/ConsumeFieldValue — self-contained, no skipValue dependency. All tests pass; conformance 700/2.

title: Add (wiresmith.options.customtype) on message fields
description:
Follow-up to [[wiresmith-06z]], which shipped customtype for bytes and string only.

**Critical for the Mimir migration ([[wiresmith-cqa]]).** Mimir's hot path uses customtype on repeated message fields:

- `WriteRequest.timeseries`: `repeated TimeSeries [(gogoproto.customtype) = "PreallocTimeseries"]` — every metric write request.
- `TimeSeries.labels` / `Metric.labels` / `Exemplar.labels`: `repeated LabelPair [(gogoproto.customtype) = "UnsafeMutableLabel"]` — every label set, the linchpin of the zero-copy buffer-reuse pattern (see `pkg/CLAUDE.md` "Unsafe memory tricks").

Customtype on a singular message field is fundamentally different plumbing: the user-supplied type owns the entire submessage wire encoding (including any sub-tags), so the Size/Marshal/Unmarshal contract is the same but the input/output bytes are the encoded message envelope, not just a length-delimited payload.

Open questions to resolve:

- Does the user type still implement the same CustomMarshaler interface (SizeWiresmith/MarshalWiresmith/UnmarshalWiresmith/EqualWiresmith), or do message customtypes need a distinct interface that signals 'I own the inner tags too'?
- How does the generator distinguish a bytes-customtype from a message-customtype at validation time? The same Go type could legitimately be either depending on the source proto kind.
- Repeated customtype message fields (most common gogoproto pattern, e.g. labels []LabelAdapter) follow naturally — gate this on whichever interface decision the singular case lands on. **Mimir needs the repeated case at minimum.**

Mimir-side caveat (not part of this bead, but called out for context): Mimir's existing `PreallocTimeseries` and `LabelAdapter` implement gogo-shaped `Marshal`/`MarshalTo`/`MarshalToSizedBuffer`/`Unmarshal`/`Size`, not wiresmith's `*Wiresmith` methods. They will need adapter methods regardless of which interface decision lands here.

Test plan when picked up:

- Add a fixture proto with customtype on a singular message and on a repeated message.
- Wire-compat test against the equivalent non-customtype proto compiled by gogofaster.
- Round-trip via wiresmith ↔ official protobuf reading the wire bytes — confirms the customtype envelope is wire-identical to a plain submessage.
- Reject customtype-on-map until a third bead investigates that case.
notes: Implementation: customtype now applies to singular AND repeated bytes/string/message fields. Reused the existing CustomMarshaler interface — same Size/Marshal/Unmarshal/Equal/CompareWiresmith contract across all three kinds (the wire envelope is length-delimited regardless; only the bytes the user type owns differ). Added types.RepeatedCustomType for the per-element loop. Cross-option compatibility is now an explicit whitelist (customtypeCompatiblePeers in option_customtype.go); customname/jsontag compatible, pointer/stdtime not — enforced via a new FieldOption.Has() interface method.
close_reason: implemented

title: Add (wiresmith.options.customtype) field option (gogo customtype equivalent)
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Pointer semantics" section; Mimir uses gogo's `(gogoproto.customtype) = "X.Y"` extensively for types like `LabelAdapter`, custom UUID/Decimal types, etc. Confirmed needed by user 2026-05-29.

**What gogo does:** `(gogoproto.customtype) = "github.com/foo/bar.MyType"` replaces the generated Go field type with the user-supplied type. The user-supplied type must implement gogo's `Marshaler`/`Unmarshaler` shape so that wire encoding works. The field is emitted as `Foo MyType` instead of `Foo []byte` (or whatever the proto kind would imply).

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Add `(wiresmith.options.customtype) = "github.com/foo/bar.MyType"` to `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`.
- Generator emits `Foo MyType` and routes Marshal/Unmarshal through `MyType.MarshalWiresmith()`/`UnmarshalWiresmith(b []byte) error` (or the gogo shape — pick one, document it).
- Reject on fields where the proto kind cannot round-trip through the custom type's encoding (e.g. customtype on a message field needs different plumbing than on bytes).

**Migration ergonomics:** consider also accepting `(gogoproto.customtype)` as a recognized alias so Mimir doesn't need to rewrite every annotation. Decide during implementation; document the choice.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Add `proto/basic/customtype.proto` fixture with at least: customtype on bytes, customtype on string, customtype on a nested message. Add a Go type for each that satisfies the chosen interface.
- Test in `test/basic/customtype_test.go` covering round-trip + Equal.
- Reject customtype on map/oneof/repeated with a clear error (matches the existing `(wiresmith.options.pointer)` rejection style).
- Update `docs/extensions.md` to document the new option.
notes:
Clarified design (user picked 2026-06-02): (1) Custom-type interface = wiresmith reverse-write shape, NOT gogo-compatible Marshal/Unmarshal. Mimir will need adapter types. (2) PR scope = bytes + string only; customtype-on-message deferred to a follow-up bead. (3) gogoproto.customtype alias deferred (same call as jsontag — no precedent infra for embedding gogo.proto).
Shipped: (wiresmith.options.customtype) on singular bytes/string fields. Interface chosen: wiresmith reverse-write (SizeWiresmith/MarshalWiresmith/UnmarshalWiresmith/EqualWiresmith) — distinct method names so gogoproto.Marshal/Unmarshal can't be confused for it. Extension 50032. Implementation hooks: option_customtype.go (resolver+parser+validator), compiler/types/customtype.go (FieldType wrapper), option_pointer.go (fieldType/goFieldType + customtypeAlias helper), emit_getter.go (var zero T fallback for nil receiver), emit_unmarshal.go (new singular-customtype branch — was bypassing fieldType for plain bytes/string). Customtype-on-message deferred — filed follow-up bead. gogoproto.customtype alias deferred (same call as jsontag — no gogo.proto infra).
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/103
close_reason: implemented

title: Remove mktemp -d dance from generate-ours conformance step
description:
## Intent

Delete the temp-dir workaround in `Makefile:120-123` once the CLI accepts
positional `.proto` file arguments. This ticket is the canary: when it
can be closed cleanly, the protoc-ergonomic-parity work has actually
landed in user-visible form.

## Why this exists

Today the Makefile does:

```make
$(eval CONF_TMP := $(shell mktemp -d))
@cp proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto "$(CONF_TMP)/"
$(WIRESMITH) --proto_path="$(CONF_TMP)" --out=gen --module=$(MODULE)
@rm -rf "$(CONF_TMP)"
```

This exists purely because wiresmith walks all `.proto` files in
`--proto_path` and emits for every one. The conformance step wants only
`test_messages_proto3.proto`, so it isolates it.

Filing as a tracking ticket (not the implementation ticket) so the
workaround removal is explicit and the parity claim is auditable.

## What changes

Once the dependency ships:

```make
$(WIRESMITH) --proto_path=proto/conformance --out=gen --module=$(MODULE) \
  proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto
```

…with no temp-dir, no cp, no cleanup.

## Acceptance criteria

- `Makefile:120-123` reduced to a single direct invocation.
- `make generate-ours` produces an identical `gen/` tree.
- `make conformance` still passes (same 696 passing / 5 expected fails).

## Source

Identified during 2026-05-26 review of wiresmith CLI vs. protoc/vtproto/
gogoproto. The mktemp dance was the most concrete ergonomic regression
spotted in the review.
notes: Already done — Makefile conformance step now reads: $(WIRESMITH) --proto_path=proto/conformance --out=gen --module=$(MODULE) proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto (Makefile:120). Landed with PR #76 (wiresmith-7eh's positional-args feature). 'make generate-ours' produces identical gen/ output verified during that PR.
close_reason: delivered alongside wiresmith-7eh in PR #76 — the tracking ticket's acceptance criteria are all met

title: Switch to per-source-file Go output (one .pb.go per .proto)
description:
## Intent

Emit exactly one `.pb.go` per input `.proto`, named by extension swap
(`foo.proto` → `foo.pb.go`), matching protoc-gen-go's per-file emission
model. Today wiresmith aggregates all `.proto` files in a proto package
into a single Go file.

## Why this exists

The source-relative output path migration in wiresmith-ahz defines the
output as `<--out>/<source-file's-path-relative-to---proto_path>.pb.go`.
That contract is impossible to honor while the generator emits per
*package*: two `.proto` files in the same package (e.g., `common.proto`
and `types.proto` both declaring `package example.v1;`) currently produce
one shared output file, not `common.pb.go` + `types.pb.go`.

Splitting this out of wiresmith-ahz so the emission-model change is
explicit and reviewable on its own, rather than buried inside path
derivation.

## What changes

- `compiler/generator/generator.go`: the per-proto-package aggregation
  in `generateFile` (and its callers) becomes per-input-file. Cross-file
  imports within the same package must resolve via Go imports
  (same-package files in Go can reference each other's identifiers
  without imports, so this is mostly an organizational change in how
  `*FileGenerator` is keyed and how registration init blocks are
  partitioned).
- Generated registration init blocks: each `.pb.go` registers only its
  own types; the aggregate-per-package model goes away.

## Acceptance criteria

- For an input tree with `pkg/common.proto` and `pkg/types.proto` in the
  same proto package, output is `pkg/common.pb.go` + `pkg/types.pb.go`,
  not a single file.
- `TestGenerateMatchesCheckedIn` updated to reflect the new per-file
  layout.
- Cross-file type references within the same package still compile.

## Relation to other tickets

This is a precondition for wiresmith-ahz (source-relative paths). The
path-derivation work is meaningless if all files in a package still
collapse to one output.

## Source

Identified during 2026-05-26 review of wiresmith CLI vs. protoc/vtproto/
gogoproto.
notes:
Per-source-file emission is already implemented — verified by inspecting generator.go (generateFile operates per fd, outputPathFor uses filepath.Base(fd.Path())) and by running a probe: two .proto files declaring the same proto package produce two distinct .pb.go + two _reflect.pb.go files, each with its own init() and file_*_rawDesc. Cross-file refs in the same Go package resolve via Go's same-package namespace and compile cleanly. Added TestGeneratePerSourceFileOutput as a regression pin so this contract can't silently regress and break the wiresmith-ahz source-relative path migration.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/75
close_reason: Already implemented; added regression test pinning per-source-file emission (acceptance-criteria check).

title: Accept .proto file paths as positional arguments to scope generation
description:
## Intent

Match protoc's positional-argument convention: `wiresmith [flags] [files...]`
compiles only the listed `.proto` files, while keeping `--proto_path` for
import resolution. When no files are given, fall back to today's recursive
walk for backward compatibility.

## Why this exists

Every peer tool (protoc, vtproto, gogoproto) takes `.proto` file paths as
positional arguments to scope generation. Wiresmith currently has no way to
say "compile this one file" — it walks the entire `--proto_path` directory.

The visible cost lives in `Makefile:120-123`:

```make
$(eval CONF_TMP := $(shell mktemp -d))
@cp proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto "$(CONF_TMP)/"
$(WIRESMITH) --proto_path="$(CONF_TMP)" --out=gen --module=$(MODULE)
@rm -rf "$(CONF_TMP)"
```

This `mktemp -d && cp` dance exists solely because wiresmith cannot be
told to compile a single file out of a multi-file directory tree. It is
the most visible smoke test for whether wiresmith's CLI ergonomics match
protoc's: when the workaround can be deleted, parity is real.

## What changes

- `cmd/wiresmith/main.go`: accept `flag.Args()` after `flag.Parse()` as
  the list of files to compile. Each argument is resolved relative to the
  current working directory (mirroring protoc).
- `compiler/generator/generator.go`: `Generator.Generate` gains the ability
  to scope compilation to a subset of files while still resolving imports
  via the full `--proto_path` walk. The internal `buildImportMapping`
  function already walks the tree; only the consumer-side "files to emit"
  filter is new.
- Empty positional args list = today's behavior (walk and emit everything).

## Acceptance criteria

- `wiresmith --proto_path=proto proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto`
  emits only `test_messages_proto3.pb.go`, not the whole tree.
- The Makefile conformance step (wiresmith-DDDD; see linked ticket) can
  drop the temp-dir dance.
- No regression in `make generate-ours` for the existing directory-mode
  invocations.

## Source

Identified during 2026-05-26 review of wiresmith CLI vs. protoc/vtproto/
gogoproto. Same review that produced wiresmith-j0h, wiresmith-ahz,
wiresmith-gz4, wiresmith-vy9.
notes:
Implemented as Generator.Files filter (filesystem-path → fd.Path() map via buildImportMapping). Empty Files keeps the recursive-walk default; non-empty filters emission only — import resolution still uses the full --proto_path walk. Positional paths outside --proto_path are rejected up-front (clearer than silently emitting nothing). Makefile's conformance step dropped its mktemp -d/cp/rm dance and now invokes wiresmith with proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto as a positional arg. Docs updated in docs/cli.md.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/76
close_reason: implemented: Generator.Files filter + CLI positional args; Makefile conformance step uses the new syntax

title: Stable public import path for protohelpers (github.com/grafana/wiresmith/protohelpers)
description:
**Source:** FLAGS.md (originally framed as "publish protohelpers as a standalone Go module"). Rescoped 2026-06-03 — the inlining work has cut the runtime surface to ~217 LOC and two consumed symbols, so the problem is now just "pick a stable Go import path the generator emits unconditionally," not "ship a separate library."

## What changed since the original framing

Recent inlining (commits 9fa2fe6 packed-varint, 4080e75 tag/length 1-byte fast path, 594501d varint/fixed decode) eliminated `SizeOfVarint`, `Skip`, `ErrInvalidLength`, `ErrIntOverflow`, `SizeOfZigzag` from wiresmith-generated `.pb.go` files (0 call sites). What is still imported from `gen/protohelpers/`:

| Symbol | Sites in wiresmith-generated code | Hot? |
|--------|----------------------------------:|------|
| `EncodeVarint` | 622 | yes — multi-statement loop, not inlinable per CLAUDE.md "rejected approaches" |
| `NewMessageReflect` | 100 | no — cold reflection adapter for `proto.Marshal`/`Unmarshal` interop |

If `wiresmith-2dy` (inline EncodeVarint body into generated code) also lands, only `NewMessageReflect` and friends remain — pure cold reflection glue.

## Intent (recovered)

The intended public path is `github.com/grafana/wiresmith/protohelpers` — a subpackage of the wiresmith repo itself, not a separate module. That was the lost-in-translation bit: the bead has been read as "extract to its own repo + publish independently," but the real ask is "give the helpers a stable, importable Go path so consumers (Tempo, Mimir, anyone else) can resolve the generated `import` statement."

The repo already lives at `github.com/grafana/wiresmith` (verified via `git remote`); only `go.mod` and the generator hardcodes need updating. No new repo, no external action.

## What needs to change

1. **`go.mod` module path**: `module wiresmith` → `module github.com/grafana/wiresmith`. Today the path is a local-only name and nothing outside the repo can `go get` it.
2. **Generator**: replace `fg.module + "/gen/protohelpers"` with the literal `github.com/grafana/wiresmith/protohelpers` at the 5 emit sites (`compiler/generator/emit_marshal.go:14,102`, `emit_protoreflect.go:49`, `emit_registration.go:218`, `types.go:64`). `--module` stops influencing the helpers import.
3. **Move the helpers**: `gen/protohelpers/` → `protohelpers/` (top-level subpackage of the module). Generated code under `gen/` keeps the same import — only its physical location changes.
4. **Regenerate** the in-repo `.pb.go` files so they reference the new path.
5. **In-repo imports**: any internal callers of `wiresmith/gen/protohelpers` flip to the canonical path.

## Private repo is fine for now

A public repo is not required. Consumers using `GOPRIVATE=github.com/grafana/*` and standard git auth (Tempo and Mimir already have this) can resolve the import from a private repo, pin via commit SHA, and upgrade as the prototype evolves. Tags become useful once we want versioned releases; they are not a precondition.

## This is what peer tools do

- `github.com/planetscale/vtprotobuf/protohelpers`
- `github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto`
- `google.golang.org/protobuf/runtime/protoimpl`

All live at fixed public import paths under the project's canonical module — exactly what this bead asks for.

## Acceptance

- `go.mod` declares `module github.com/grafana/wiresmith`.
- Generator emits the fixed `github.com/grafana/wiresmith/protohelpers` import; `--module` flag is decoupled from helpers (still used elsewhere for now; full removal tracked under `wiresmith-j0h`).
- An external Go project (e.g., a throwaway scratch repo with `GOPRIVATE` set) can `go get` and import a generated wiresmith package and call `proto.Marshal` against it without any rewrites.
- `gen/protohelpers/` moved to `protohelpers/` (top-level); existing checked-in generated code updated.

**Related**: `wiresmith-2dy` (inline EncodeVarint into generated code) is a parallel-path optimization that shrinks the remaining surface to cold reflection glue. Not a blocker — this bead is about the stable path, not about whether `EncodeVarint` survives. **Supersedes (note)**: ARCH-6 (`wiresmith-88q`, closed) proposed the same thing in different framing.
notes: 2026-06-03: Reframed from "publish as standalone module" to "stable public import path under the existing wiresmith module." Inlining work has shrunk the runtime surface to 217 LOC and two consumed symbols. Private repo is acceptable for prototyping (GOPRIVATE + SHA pinning); public release can come later. No external action needed — the repo is already at github.com/grafana/wiresmith (verified via git remote). The earlier "blocked: requires creating a new public Go module repo" premise was wrong; unblocking.
close_reason: Module renamed to github.com/grafana/wiresmith; protohelpers moved to top-level package; generator hardcodes the canonical import path; all tests pass. Private repo + GOPRIVATE on consumer side is sufficient (Mimir already has GOPRIVATE=github.com/grafana/* in .config/go/env).

title: Drop the opentelemetry.proto.* special-case in goPackageDir
description:
**Source:** FLAGS.md

### 3. Drop the `opentelemetry.proto.*` special-case

Once `go_package` is the sole authority, the `otlp/` prefix shortcut in
`goPackageDir` is dead code. The OTLP protos already set
`option go_package = "go.opentelemetry.io/proto/otlp/common/v1"`, which
under the new model is exactly what determines their output location.
Remove the special-case branch.
notes: Blocked on wiresmith-gz4 → wiresmith-ahz (FLAGS.md steps 1+2 must land first). Removing the OTLP special-case in isolation breaks codegen: today the OTLP go_package value 'go.opentelemetry.io/proto/otlp/common/v1' is NOT under effectiveBase=wiresmith/gen, so resolveGoPackage returns ok=false and destFor falls through to goPackageDir, which uses the special-case to map opentelemetry.proto.common.v1 → otlp/common/v1. Without the special-case AND without step-2's unconditional-honor-go_package change, the OTLP files would land at gen/opentelemetry/proto/common/v1/ instead of gen/otlp/common/v1/. Defer per the FLAGS.md migration plan.
close_reason: Already fixed in code — verified during session audit (see commit message for per-bead evidence)

title: Always honor go_package; require it when imports cross packages
description:
**Source:** FLAGS.md

### 2. Always honor `go_package`; require it when imports cross packages

`resolveGoPackage` currently gates `go_package` behind "is the import path
under our `effectiveBase`?". Drop that gate. If a proto file sets
`option go_package`, that string is the generated file's import path,
unconditionally. Cross-file imports between wiresmith-generated files read
each other's `go_package` directly, the same way protoc does.

For a single-file generation with no cross-file imports, `go_package` can
be omitted and the import path is derived from `--out` + source-relative
path + the consumer's Go module root. (Or we can require `go_package`
always, matching protoc-gen-go's strictness — easier to reason about.)
notes: Scope question for next claimant: literal reading 'honor go_package unconditionally' breaks wiresmith's own OTel build, because the vendored OTel protos' go_package = 'go.opentelemetry.io/...' would become the import path but the file lives on disk at wiresmith/gen/opentelemetry/proto/.... Three paths forward to discuss with user: (a) drop the gate + add 'replace' directive in go.mod, (b) add CLI M-flag override (like vtproto/gogoproto Makefile already does at the protoc level) + drop the gate, (c) narrow to 'drop the gate when go_package matches the source-relative dest' — effectively a no-op vs. ahz's relaxed-but-keeps-build-green behavior. User input needed before claiming.
close_reason: Closed

title: Use source-relative output paths (drop proto-package-based path derivation)
description:
**Source:** FLAGS.md

### 1. Drop proto-package-based path derivation; use source-relative paths

Remove `effectiveBase` and `goPackageDir` from
`compiler/generator/names.go`. The output path becomes:

```
<--out>/<source-file's-path-relative-to---proto_path>.pb.go
```

Concretely, for input `pkg/.patched-proto/common/v1/common.proto` invoked
with `--proto_path=pkg/.patched-proto --out=pkg/tempopb`, the output is
`pkg/tempopb/common/v1/common.pb.go`. This is the `paths=source_relative`
contract.
notes:
Implementation: source-relative output path drives the on-disk location (outDir + filepath.Dir(fd.Path()) + basename). Go import-path resolution still goes through destFor with go_package honored under the legacy module/gen base; the always-honor-go_package change is wiresmith-gz4's territory. Decision: did NOT restructure vendored proto/otlp/ — instead let gen/otlp/* relocate to gen/opentelemetry/proto/* and updated 16 consumer files. Trade-off chosen so the upstream OTel protos stay byte-identical to their source.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/78
close_reason: implemented: source-relative output paths; effectiveBase and goPackageDir removed; gen/otlp/* relocated to gen/opentelemetry/proto/*; consumers updated

title: Eliminate --strip_prefix, --import_base, --helpers_import (umbrella)
description:
**Source:** FLAGS.md

# Proposal: Eliminate `--strip_prefix`, `--import_base`, `--helpers_import`

[TEMPO.md](TEMPO.md) lists three CLI flags as planned additions for the Tempo
integration:

```
--strip_prefix    Proto package prefix to strip for Go paths (e.g. "tempopb")
--import_base     Go import path base (replaces module/gen/ prefix)
--helpers_import  Import path for protohelpers package
```

Other Go protobuf compilers — `protoc-gen-go`, `protoc-gen-gogofaster`,
`protoc-gen-go-vtproto` — don't have flags like this. They get the same
configurability from two existing protoc-native mechanisms: the
`paths=source_relative` output mode and a fixed, published runtime helpers
import path. This proposal eliminates the three flags by adopting both.

## Why the flags exist

Wiresmith currently derives output locations from the proto **package**, not the
source **path**. In `compiler/generator/names.go`:

```go
func effectiveBase(module string) string {
    return module + "/gen"
}

func goPackageDir(protoPkg string) string {
    parts := strings.Split(protoPkg, ".")
    if len(parts) >= 3 && parts[0] == "opentelemetry" && parts[1] == "proto" {
        return "otlp/" + strings.Join(parts[2:], "/")
    }
    return strings.Join(parts, "/")
}
```

`destFor` builds the output directory from `effectiveBase(module) + goPackageDir(protoPkg)`,
and an `option go_package` is only honored when it falls under
`effectiveBase`. Two hardcodes are baked into this:

1. The `/gen` suffix on `effectiveBase` — needed `--import_base` to override.
2. The `opentelemetry.proto.*` → `otlp/...` special case in `goPackageDir`,
   plus the dotted-package-as-path fallback — needed `--strip_prefix` to
   re-shape for non-OTel package layouts.

The `protohelpers` import is hardcoded similarly in
`compiler/generator/emit_registration.go` and `types.go`:

```go
fg.imports.addImport(fg.module+"/gen/protohelpers", "")
```

— needed `--helpers_import` to override.

## How protoc-based plugins solve the same problem

### Output path: `paths=source_relative`

protoc-gen-go (and every plugin that wraps protoc) supports
`paths=source_relative`. The output location of a generated file is purely
`<--go_out>/<source-relative-path>.pb.go`. The proto package is *not*
consulted to compute the path.

Cross-file imports between generated files are resolved from each input
file's `option go_package`. The `go_package` is the single source of truth
for "what import path does this generated file have"; the on-disk location
just has to agree with it because Go's module layout requires it.

For Tempo this means: a patched proto at
`pkg/.patched-proto/common/v1/common.proto` with
`option go_package = "github.com/grafana/tempo/pkg/tempopb/common/v1";` and
`--go_out=pkg/tempopb --go_opt=paths=source_relative` produces
`pkg/tempopb/common/v1/common.pb.go`. No `gen/` in the path, no per-package
flags. The proto file's own metadata fully specifies the layout.

### Runtime helpers: fixed module path

vtprotobuf publishes its runtime support at the stable Go module path
`github.com/planetscale/vtprotobuf/protohelpers`. Generated code imports
that path verbatim. There is no per-codegen flag for "where do helpers
live" because the answer is always the same: at the published module path.

Consumers don't vendor helpers under their own tree; they take a Go module
dependency like any other library.

## Migration path for existing wiresmith consumers

The wiresmith codebase itself uses `--module=wiresmith --out=gen` and
relies on `effectiveBase = "wiresmith/gen"` plus the OTel special-case to
produce `gen/otlp/common/v1/common.pb.go` from
`package opentelemetry.proto.common.v1`. Under this proposal, those protos
need `option go_package = "wiresmith/gen/otlp/common/v1";` (or wherever
wiresmith wants them to land) and invocation drops `--module`.

This is a one-time edit per generated proto package in the wiresmith repo,
done at the same commit that lands the codegen change. After that, the
codegen layer has no module-level configuration at all.

## Resulting consumer experience

Tempo's invocation becomes:

```bash
wiresmith \
  --proto_path=pkg/.patched-proto \
  --out=pkg/tempopb
```

— with each patched proto setting its `option go_package` to
`github.com/grafana/tempo/pkg/tempopb/<X>/v1` (which Tempo's `gen-proto`
make target already does for the gogofast pass). The `--module`,
`--strip_prefix`, `--import_base`, and `--helpers_import` flags all
disappear; the only remaining flags are `--proto_path` and `--out`, both of
which match protoc's `--proto_path` / `--go_out` and need no explanation
to anyone who has used protoc before.

Generated files land at `pkg/tempopb/common/v1/common.pb.go` directly —
no `gen/` subdirectory and no 101-file import rewrite of Tempo consumers,
which is the diff-pollution cost of the current workaround documented in
[TEMPO.md](TEMPO.md).
notes:
## Strategic context (added 2026-05-26)

This umbrella simplifies the standalone `cmd/wiresmith` CLI. The BUF-EPIC
(wiresmith-c4r) is a parallel track adding `cmd/protoc-gen-wiresmith` as a
protoc plugin. Implementers should be aware that two surfaces will coexist
unless/until a decision is made to deprecate one:

- Today: `cmd/wiresmith` is the only entry point.
- After this umbrella lands: standalone CLI matches protoc flag idioms
  (paths=source_relative, go_package as truth) but is still
  directory-walking, not plugin-style.
- After BUF-EPIC lands: `cmd/protoc-gen-wiresmith` exists alongside, usable
  from `buf generate`. Standalone CLI is still maintained.
- Possible future: deprecate standalone CLI once plugin is stable; the
  plugin route is what all peer tools (protoc-gen-go, vtproto, gogoproto)
  expose exclusively.

Do not make changes in this umbrella that would prevent the plugin track,
and vice versa. The generator core (descriptor-in, files-out) should remain
the shared substrate for both surfaces.
close_reason: All four sub-beads (wiresmith-ick, -ahz, -vy9, -gz4) closed; the three flags never landed because the sub-beads aligned wiresmith with protoc idioms (paths=source_relative + option go_package + fixed protohelpers import path), eliminating the need for them.

title: DOC-4: TEMPO.md documents flags that do not exist
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `TEMPO.md:6-23`.

**What:** Promises `--strip_prefix`, `--import_base`, `--helpers_import`. None exist in `cmd/wiresmith/main.go`. `FLAGS.md` (untracked) proposes *not* adding them. Users following TEMPO.md will see `flag provided but not defined: -strip_prefix`.

**Fix:** Either commit `FLAGS.md` and link to it as the resolution, or rewrite `TEMPO.md` to say "proposed; superseded by FLAGS.md". Don't leave contradictory truth claims in the tree.

title: DOC-3: (wiresmith.options.pointer) field option undocumented
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** missing from `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `MIMIR.md`, `TEMPO.md`, `BUF.md`, `FLAGS.md`.

**What:** PR #59 added the public extension option. Users must `import "wiresmith/options.proto"` in their own protos. Only references today are the test fixture (`proto/basic/pointer.proto`) and a source comment in `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`. CLAUDE.md "Keep documentation in sync" caveat applies directly.

**Fix:** Add "Custom options" section to README and `AGENTS.md` "Design decisions" with: import path, no-vendoring guarantee, syntax (`(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true`), what it does (`*T`/`[]*T`), where it's invalid (optional/oneof/map/scalar).

Added 'Custom field options' section to AGENTS.md pointing at docs/extensions.md; updated MIMIR.md 'Pointer semantics' entry to show (gogoproto.nullable) has shipped as (wiresmith.options.pointer). README.md already linked docs/extensions.md (PR #71). docs/extensions.md was already comprehensive; TEMPO/BUF/FLAGS.md are not about field-level options so skipped.

title: SEC-5: Cross-package message unmarshal resets recursion-depth counter
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:167-169` (public `Unmarshal` wrapper) + `compiler/types/type.go:236-242` (`emitUnmarshalCall`).

**What:** Same-package nested messages call `.unmarshal(b, depth+1)`; cross-package nested messages call `.Unmarshal(b)` which restarts depth at 0. A graph bouncing between packages can recurse to `maxUnmarshalDepth × pkgCount` levels. For OTel today the package count is small enough to be safe, but the protection silently weakens as packages multiply. Documented contract ("unmarshal depth is bounded") is broken across the boundary.

**Fix:** Give every message a single internal entry like `unmarshalWithDepth(b, depth)` and have cross-package callers invoke it via an exported wrapper that accepts the counter, or wrap depth in a context-style parameter. Concrete sketch: emit `UnmarshalWithDepth(b []byte, depth int) error` as the cross-package surface and call it from `emitUnmarshalCall` when `!isSamePackage`.
notes:
Implemented: emit a public UnmarshalWithDepth(b, depth) method on every generated message; cross-package unmarshal sites now route through it with depth+1 instead of the depth-resetting public Unmarshal(b). Three call sites changed: emit_unmarshal.go's oneof-message and optional-message branches, plus the shared emitUnmarshalCall helper in compiler/types/type.go. TestGenerateCrossPackageUnmarshalThreadsDepth pins the codegen contract; TestUnmarshalWithDepthRespectsStartingDepth pins the runtime contract. Documented in docs/generated-api.md. All 696 conformance tests still pass (regenerated gen/ + ran tests; no diff in gen/ output beyond the new method).
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/77
close_reason: implemented: cross-package unmarshal now threads depth via UnmarshalWithDepth

title: TEST-3: Add test for field number 0 rejection in unmarshal
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** missing — should live under `test/basic/wire_validation_test.go`.

**What:** `type.go:169` emits `if X>>3 < 1 || X>>3 > 0x1FFFFFFF { return ... }`. CLAUDE.md lists this as a recurring caveat. A regression would be silent today.

**Fix:** One table-driven test: feed `[]byte{0x00, ...}` (tag = field 0, wire type 0) through every constructor in `testutil.AllMessageConstructors()` and assert a non-nil error containing "invalid field number".
notes:
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/74
Implementation note: switched from AllMessageConstructors() (per the original Fix text) to AllPanicSafeConstructors() in response to PR #74 review — the test asserts only on the returned error, so map-bearing types are safe to include and bring the actual coverage up to the documented 'every generated Unmarshal' claim.
close_reason: PR #74 merged (commit 099309d on main: 'test: pin field-0 tag rejection across every generated Unmarshal'); status was open due to a sync miss

title: DOC-2: README Usage omits install + actual CLI invocation
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `README.md:88-97`.

**What:** Only "Usage" content is `make generate/build/test/bench`. No `go install`, no example `wiresmith --proto_path=... --out=...`, no mention of `--version` or `--help`. Reader cannot use the binary outside this repo's Makefile.

**Fix:** Add "Install" section with `go install wiresmith/cmd/wiresmith@latest` and "Run" with the three real flags. Reference `--help` and `--version`.
notes:
Implementation deviates from bead's suggested 'go install wiresmith/cmd/wiresmith@latest' because the project's module path is bare 'wiresmith' (no host prefix) — go install would try to fetch 'wiresmith' as a remote module and fail. Followed docs/cli.md instead: git clone + go build.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/79
close_reason: implemented: README now has Install (build-from-source) and Run sections with the real CLI invocation; --help and --version referenced

title: CR-6: Size() and Marshal* panic on nil; Get*/Equal are nil-safe
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_size.go:15`; `emit_marshal.go:17,27,34`.

**What:** Inconsistent nil-safety contract. Users who learn "wiresmith methods are nil-safe" from `Get*`/`Has*`/`Equal`/`Reset`/`String` will be surprised when `nil.Size()` panics. Either tighten the contract universally or document the cutoff explicitly.

**Fix:** Emit `if m == nil { return 0 }` for `Size()` and `if m == nil { return nil, nil }` (or `(0, nil)`) for the `Marshal*` family. Cost: one branch in cold path. Alternative: explicitly document `Size`/`Marshal` as nil-unsafe in CLAUDE.md.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: SEC-4: Inline varint decoder lacks 10-byte boundary overflow check
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `compiler/types/type.go:174-185` (`emitConsumeVarintAt`), `216-232` (`emitConsumeBytesLenAt`); skip path in `emit_unmarshal.go:31-40`.

**What:** The loop terminates on `shift >= 64` (next iteration), so iteration 10 still runs with `shift == 63` and does `v |= uint64(b & 0x7F) << 63`. Bits 1–6 of the 10th byte silently overflow past the uint64 boundary. The wire-format spec requires that on the 10th byte, `b & 0x7F` MUST be ≤ 1. CLAUDE.md flags this exact requirement. The tag decoder (line 157-170) is fine — its 5-byte cap + field-number range check at line 169 cover it.

**Fix:** After reading `b` inside the loop, emit `if shift == 63 && b > 1 { return fmt.Errorf("proto: varint overflow") }`. Apply to both `emitConsumeVarintAt` and `emitConsumeBytesLenAt`. Cheap; one branch in the cold path.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: TEST-2: Add unit tests for 22 untested compiler/types/*.go files
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** missing — `compiler/types/{bool,bytes,double,float,fixed*,sint*,int*,uint*,varint,map,message,oneof,optional,pointer,repeated*,string}_test.go`.

**What:** Only `equal_test.go` exists. The other `Type` interface methods (`EmitSize`, `EmitMarshal`, `EmitUnmarshal`, `EmitValueSize`, `EmitValueMarshal`, `CastExpr`, `IsPackable`, `WireType`, `SizeByIndex`, `FixedSize`, `VarintSizeExpr`, `ZeroLiteral`) are per-kind invariants that should be locked down individually.

**Fix:** Extend the `captureEmitter` pattern from `equal_test.go`. Highest-value cases:
  - `bytes_test.go`: confirms `EmitUnmarshal` uses `append([]byte(nil), ...)` (bytes-aliasing guard).
  - `map_test.go`: confirms merge-of-empty-but-present message; confirms key/value tag ordering.
